item.title: July 4th celebrations mark America's 250th birthday. Follow live updates.
item.description: Americans are celebrating 250 years of independence this Fourth of July with events including the largest fireworks display in history.

item.title: Mourners chant for revenge as dayslong funeral of Iran's slain leader begins
item.description: The flag-draped casket of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was put on display in Tehran with millions expected to attend his dayslong funeral.

item.title: Prince Harry to travel solo to London amid security flap
item.description: Meghan and his children may eventually join him on the rest of the trip outside London, the source said.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 5)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Punch Brothers perform "New Bike"
item.description: Bluegrass band Punch Brothers was formed by mandolinist Chris Thile in 2006. Their upcoming all-instrumental album, "The Unsung Adventures of Punch Brothers," will be released on July 24. Here's Punch Brothers performing "New Bike."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Punch Brothers perform "This Land Is Your Land"
item.description: Bluegrass band Punch Brothers was formed by mandolinist Chris Thile in 2006. In celebration of the Fourth of July, here's Punch Brothers performing their cover of Woody Guthrie's classic song, "This Land Is Your Land."

item.title: Who were the last residents of Liberty Island?
item.description: The Statue of Liberty is one of the most recognized works of art in the world, symbolizing American progress and the welcoming promise of immigration. Lady Liberty stands on a 15-acre island, now crowded by tourists — but once, a small group of residents called the island their home.

item.title: Serena Williams withdraws from Wimbledon doubles match with sister Venus
item.description: Serena Williams cited a knee injury behind her decision to withdraw from a doubles match at Wimbledon.

item.title: Flying "Old Glory" and the history of America's most iconic flags
item.description: The iconic American flag is recognized around the world, representing the ideals and image of the nation. Author and photographer Roland Miller joins "CBS Saturday Mornings" to showcase some of the most famous flag moments in American history.

item.title: DNA technology leads to identification of Revolutionary War soldier
item.description: The remains of a Revolutionary War soldier were identified as a young man from Maryland just before America's 250th anniversary.

item.title: Philadelphia's Reading Terminal Market offers feast of food, history
item.description: Reading Terminal Market in Philadelphia is just blocks from the location of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. "CBS Saturday Mornings" takes a stroll through this historic market.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV celebrates America's 250th birthday, receives Liberty Medal
item.description: Chicago-born Pope Leo XIV, the first American pontiff, celebrated America's 250th birthday with a speech, praising the nation's tradition of welcoming immigrants. The National Constitution Center also awarded the pope with a Liberty Medal.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce tie the knot at Madison Square Garden
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce were married at Madison Square Garden in New York City on Friday. Comedian Adam Sandler officiated the ceremony, a representative for Swift confirmed. Jo Ling Kent has more details.

item.title: Revolutionary War soldier's identity revealed after 250 years
item.description: After 250 years, a soldier who fought for the birth of the nation has finally been identified and recognized for his service. Bradley Blackburn has more.

item.title: Egypt uncovers lost Byzantine-era city in the western desert
item.description: The fourth-century residential city in the western desert is one of two major archaeological finds announced by Egypt on Saturday.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Philadelphia buries time capsule to commemorate America's 250th birthday
item.description: To commemorate America's 250th birthday, Philadelphia will bury a time capsule on the same grounds where the Founding Fathers signed the Declaration of Independence.

item.title: Scorching heat disrupts some Fourth of July celebrations
item.description: Washington, D.C., and Philadelphia have called off some celebratory events amid searing heat. Intense storms in the Midwest and a shark sighting in New York also disrupted holiday activities. Lana Zak reports.

item.title: Bessent on Trump Accounts, gas prices and affordability
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent spoke exclusively with Kelly O'Grady about inflation, gas prices and Trump Accounts, which are now available.

item.title: Trump celebrates America 250 with political messaging and patriotic pageantry
item.description: President Trump is teasing a massive fireworks display in Washington, D.C., as the grand finale to a series of Independence Day celebrations. This comes as Trump presses Congress to pass his national voter ID legislation, the SAVE America Act. CBS News' Taurean Small reports.

item.title: 7/4: CBS Saturday Morning
item.description: Americans are celebrating the Fourth of July and America's 250th birthday amid a sweltering heat wave. Plus, Taylor Swift's wedding, a Revolutionary War soldier is finally recognized, and former Liberty Island residents speak out.

item.title: 1 symptom leads to dad's cancer diagnosis: "I was like, 'Wait, what?'"
item.description: Eric Dillon thought the pain in his shoulder was a minor injury. It took two years to get the real answer.

item.title: Family-owned company prepares to put on the largest fireworks display in history
item.description: When the sun goes down on Independence Day, the skies of Washington, D.C., are expected to fill with a record-setting 850,000 individual fireworks for a 40-minute spectacle like no one has seen before.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce married in ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce were married as they celebrated their wedding with hundreds of guests Friday at Madison Square Garden in New York City.

item.title: Trump warns of "communist menace" in speech at Mount Rushmore
item.description: The president kicked off America's 250th anniversary celebrations with a speech at Mount Rushmore where he warned of a resurgence in communism.

item.title: 6 presidential speeches that shaped American history
item.description: These six presidential speeches are some that have most reverberated through the ages, and whose impacts are still felt today.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Empire State Building lights up for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding
item.description: The Empire State Building lit up in blue for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding Friday night.

item.title: Maps show this July 4th could be hottest ever in parts of U.S.
item.description: Extremely dangerous heat, coupled with humidity, could result in heat index readings of 100 to 115 degrees from the Midwest to the East Coast, forecasters said.

item.title: Trump announces pardons for pollution violators prosecuted for "fixing their car"
item.description: CBS News previously reported President Trump was weighing pardons of a slate of people convicted of emissions and clean air-related violations.
